trigger_incident.summary: Long-running launchd-driven fingerprint campaign was anchored to the canonical repo path instead of a feature/ worktree. Wrapper's relative writes resolved against launchd's WorkingDirectory; canonical jsonl landed in the wrong tree; required mid-flight isolation + restart + remerge before clean data resumed.

supporting: Extension to `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` skill: auto-detect when a feature with an active branch is being touched from main and prompt/auto-create the worktree

Pre-state.json-mutation guard for scheduled jobs: verify `branch + cwd + WorkingDirectory` resolve to the expected `.worktrees/<name>` (or sibling `/Volumes/DevSSD/FitTracker2-*`) tree before any write
`make verify-isolation` readout listing every active feature with its expected branch + worktree path + actual git/launchd state
Documentation of which jobs/skills are exempt (e.g. framework-level meta-work that legitimately runs on main)

**(added 2026-05-07)** Companion gate `FEATURE_CLOSURE_COMPLETENESS` (write-time + readout-time) — fires on commits that transition `current_phase` → `complete` with a populated `case_study` link. Validates the state.json ↔ case study cross-reference is fully populated: every required frontmatter field (`date_written`, `dispatch_pattern`, `success_metrics`, `kill_criteria`, `framework_version`, `work_type`, `tier_tags_present`) is present and non-empty; state.json's PR list (`tasks[].pr_number`, `phases.merge.pr_number`) is cited in the case study; numeric claims carry T1/T2/T3 tier tags inline or in `key_numbers`. Promotes the 5 documentation-debt fields from advisory to write-time gate. Trigger memory: 2026-05-07 reconcile session that surfaced 5 missing frontmatter fields on UCC + import-training-plan AFTER both features had shipped (current detection via `make documentation-debt` runs only post-hoc).

**(added 2026-05-07)** Companion readout `make feature-completeness-audit` — system-wide scan across every `.claude/features/*/state.json` and every `docs/case-studies/*.md`. For terminal features: full FEATURE_CLOSURE_COMPLETENESS check. For in-progress features: phase-appropriate schema check (e.g. PRD phase requires `success_metrics` in PRD; Implementation phase requires `tasks[]` populated). Outputs punch list grouped by feature. Replaces the manual cross-reference reconcile pass we just ran on 2026-05-07.

decisions.decision: Two modes by work classification: (a) infra/framework/hub work → Mode B (every commit on framework-internal paths) with auto-isolation; (b) non-infra features → Mode C (current_phase mutations only) with per-feature opt-out. Infra paths: .githooks/*, .github/workflows/*, scripts/*, .claude/skills/*, .claude/shared/*, CLAUDE.md, docs/architecture/*, Makefile, plus any state.json with work_subtype framework_feature or work_type chore.

Option A — auto-invoke superpowers:using-git-worktrees skill for both modes. Skill's smart-directory selection picks placement (FitTracker2-{feature} for features, FitTracker2-infra-{shortname} for infra). Commit blocked; user re-stages from inside the new worktree.

Per-feature opt-out via state.json::isolation_opt_out: true (default false). Mandatory state.json::isolation_opt_out_reason when opt-out is true. INFRA OVERRIDE: infra/framework work ignores isolation_opt_out (the framework cannot opt itself out of its own enforcement). Operator can use `git commit --no-verify` for true emergency bypass; this is recorded in Mechanism A coverage as `manual_bypass`.

decisions.topic: launchd plist inspection vs process cwd?
decisions.decision: Primary (mandatory, cross-platform): process cwd check at write-time via Python wrapper around state.json/log writes. Reject when os.getcwd() doesn't match expected worktree (resolved from state.json::branch + worktree registry). Secondary (optional, macOS-only, cycle-time only): scan ~/Library/LaunchAgents/*.plist for misconfigurations. Flag as advisory `BRANCH_ISOLATION_LAUNCHD_DRIFT` (does not block). Skipped on Linux/CI.

decisions.topic: Cycle-time audit for 'all on main' history?
decisions.decision: Both — defense in depth. Write-time mirror (Q4 primary) + cycle-time scan auditing `.claude/features/*/state.json` against `git log --all --oneline -- path/to/feature/` to detect features whose entire git history happened on main. Flagged as advisory `BRANCH_ISOLATION_HISTORICAL`. Forward-only: applies only to features with created_at >= ship_date of this gate.

Bidirectional. (a) state.json → case study: every PR cited in tasks[].pr_number / phases.merge.pr_number / tasks[].related_prs must appear in case study body (regex match `PR #N` / `pull/N`) or `related_prs` frontmatter. (b) case study → state.json: every PR referenced in the case study via the same regex must appear in state.json's PR list. Mismatches block closure commit. Override: pr_citation_exempt frontmatter listing exempted PR numbers with reason.

Yes, required + pre-merge-review check. (a) Pre-commit gate: if kill_criteria non-empty in case study frontmatter on current_phase=complete, then kill_criteria_resolution must also be non-empty. Empty → block. (b) /ux pre-merge-review and /design pre-merge-review skills extended: if kill_criteria non-empty, verify kill_criteria_resolution is non-empty AND substantively addresses each kill threshold (heuristic: mentions at least 1 of the kill thresholds OR contains words 'not tripped' / 'deferred' / 'superseded'). Failure → state.json::pre_merge_review.{ux,design}: blocked.
